content: Rate limiting in Next.js serverless pitfalls: (1) CRITICAL: In-memory rate limiters (Map/LRU) do not work in serverless — each invocation is isolated, attackers distribute across instances to bypass; (2) Cold starts reset in-memory state entirely; (3) Must use external distributed store: Upstash Redis (serverless-compatible, edge-cacheable) or Vercel KV; (4) Neon Postgres as rate limit store adds latency (50-100ms per check) and connection overhead — not recommended for hot paths; (5) X-Forwarded-For header trivially spoofable for IP-based rate limiting unless trusted proxy headers are validated; (6) Missing rate limits on specific endpoints: forgot-password, verify-email, and 2FA endpoints are common oversights beyond login; (7) Sliding window algorithms preferred over fixed window to prevent burst attacks at window boundaries.
